description: Verifies that Claude applies the correct stack standards (NestJS/Next.js) when generating code. Each eval presents a coding task and checks that the output respects the active STACK.md rules.

evals.prompt: Necesito crear un endpoint POST /api/invoices que reciba { clientId, amount, dueDate } y guarde la factura en la base de datos. El proyecto usa NestJS + Drizzle.
evals.expected_output: El código debe: (1) usar un repository para las queries de Drizzle, NO llamar Drizzle directamente desde el service, (2) usar createZodDto para el DTO del request, (3) tener @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard) o @Public(), (4) usar el DomainExceptionFilter en lugar de throw new HttpException, (5) tener @ApiOperation y @ApiResponse en el controller.

assertions.name: usa_repository_layer
assertions.description: El service no importa ni llama Drizzle directamente — delega al repository
assertions.check: output does NOT contain direct drizzle/db calls inside a .service.ts file
assertions.pass_when: Output contains a .repository.ts file or references an injected repository. No `db.` or `drizzle(` calls appear inside *.service.ts code blocks.
assertions.fail_when: Output contains `db.select(`, `db.insert(`, `drizzle(` or similar inside a *.service.ts file without a repository intermediary.

assertions.name: usa_zod_dto
assertions.description: El DTO usa createZodDto o z.infer, no una interface manual
assertions.check: output contains createZodDto or z.infer for the request body type
assertions.pass_when: Output contains `createZodDto(` or `z.infer<` applied to the request DTO class or type.
assertions.fail_when: Output defines the request body as a plain TypeScript interface or class with manually typed properties, without using createZodDto or z.infer.

assertions.name: tiene_auth_guard
assertions.description: El endpoint tiene @UseGuards o @Public decorators explícitos
assertions.pass_when: Output contains `@UseGuards(` or `@Public()` applied to the controller class or the specific endpoint method.
assertions.fail_when: Output defines the endpoint method without any @UseGuards or @Public decorator — auth is unspecified.

assertions.name: no_http_exception_directo
assertions.description: No usa new HttpException — usa DomainError o el exception filter
assertions.pass_when: Output uses a DomainError subclass (e.g. `throw new InvoiceNotFoundError(...)`) or no explicit throw at all (relying on the DomainExceptionFilter).
assertions.fail_when: Output contains `throw new HttpException(` anywhere in the service or controller.

assertions.name: tiene_swagger
assertions.description: El controller tiene @ApiOperation y al menos un @ApiResponse
assertions.pass_when: Output contains both `@ApiOperation({` and at least one `@ApiResponse({` on the endpoint method.
assertions.fail_when: Output defines the controller method without @ApiOperation or without any @ApiResponse decorator.

evals.prompt: Tengo que construir una lista de facturas en Next.js. Viene de la API GET /api/invoices. Muéstrame cómo hacer el query hook y el widget que lo usa.
evals.expected_output: Debe usar React Query (useQuery / queryOptions), NO useEffect + fetch. El widget debe estar en features/invoices/ui/widgets/, separado del query hook en features/invoices/application/queries/. No debe haber lógica de negocio en el componente.

assertions.name: usa_react_query
assertions.description: Usa React Query (useQuery, queryOptions), no useEffect + fetch
assertions.check: output contains useQuery or queryOptions and does NOT contain 'useEffect' with fetch
assertions.pass_when: Output imports and uses `useQuery` or `queryOptions` from '@tanstack/react-query'. No `useEffect` paired with `fetch` or `axios` appears in the component.
assertions.fail_when: Output contains a `useEffect(() => { fetch('/api/invoices')` pattern or any manual fetch-in-effect pattern inside the component.

assertions.name: estructura_de_capas
assertions.description: El hook está en application/queries/, el componente en ui/widgets/
assertions.check: output references application/queries/ for the hook and ui/widgets/ for the component
assertions.pass_when: Output places the query hook file path inside `features/*/application/queries/` and the widget component inside `features/*/ui/widgets/`.
assertions.fail_when: Output co-locates the query hook inside the component file, or places either file outside the expected layer directories.

assertions.name: sin_logica_en_componente
assertions.description: El componente solo renderiza, no contiene lógica de transformación de datos
assertions.check: the UI component does not contain business logic — it consumes the query hook and renders
assertions.pass_when: The widget component only calls the query hook and maps results to JSX. No data transformation, filtering, sorting, or business calculations appear in the component body.
assertions.fail_when: The widget component contains array methods like `.filter()`, `.reduce()`, `.map()` with business logic, or inline data transformation before rendering.

evals.expected_output: El review debe identificar como Critical o Required: (1) Drizzle llamado directamente en el service (debe ir en un repository), (2) uso de HttpException directo (debe ser DomainError), (3) falta de logging, (4) retorno del objeto DB sin mapear a un response DTO.

assertions.name: detecta_drizzle_en_service
assertions.description: El review identifica que Drizzle no debe llamarse directamente en el service
assertions.check: review flags direct DB/Drizzle call in service as a required change
assertions.pass_when: Review explicitly flags `this.db.insert(` or the direct DrizzleService injection as a violation. Mentions that DB access must go through a repository layer.
assertions.fail_when: Review does not mention the direct Drizzle call in the service, or mentions it only as a suggestion/nit without flagging it as a required change.

assertions.name: detecta_http_exception
assertions.description: El review flags HttpException como red flag del stack
assertions.check: review mentions HttpException as a stack violation or red flag
assertions.pass_when: Review explicitly calls out `throw new HttpException(` as a stack violation and recommends using a DomainError subclass instead.
assertions.fail_when: Review does not mention HttpException, or mentions it as a minor style concern rather than a stack red flag.

assertions.name: usa_etiquetas_de_severidad
assertions.description: El review usa las etiquetas de severidad (Critical, Important, Nit, etc.)
assertions.check: review contains severity labels like Critical:, Important:, or Nit:
assertions.pass_when: Review output contains at least one severity label (`Critical:`, `Important:`, `Nit:`, `Optional:`, or `FYI`) applied to one or more findings.
assertions.fail_when: Review lists findings as plain bullets or numbered items without any severity label prefix.

evals.prompt: Estoy trabajando en un proyecto Go (tiene go.mod). Quiero una función que lea configuración de un archivo YAML.
evals.expected_output: Claude debe reconocer que el stack es Go y aplicar patrones Go idiomáticos. Debe mencionar que los estándares específicos de Go están en stacks/go/STACK.md como [PENDIENTE] y proceder con convenciones generales de Go (error handling explícito, no panic en producción, etc.).

assertions.name: reconoce_stack_go
assertions.description: Claude identifica el stack como Go y lo menciona explícitamente
assertions.check: response identifies or mentions Go stack
assertions.pass_when: Response contains a statement like 'Active stack: Go' or explicitly identifies the project as a Go project at the start of the response.
assertions.fail_when: Response generates Go code without first identifying or mentioning the active stack.

assertions.name: manejo_de_errores_go
assertions.description: El código usa error handling explícito de Go (if err != nil), no panic
assertions.check: output uses 'if err != nil' pattern and does NOT use panic() for error handling
assertions.pass_when: Output contains `if err != nil` for all fallible operations and does not contain `panic(` for error paths.
assertions.fail_when: Output uses `panic(err)` or `log.Fatal(err)` as the primary error handling strategy, or omits error checks entirely.

assertions.name: menciona_pendiente
assertions.description: Si aplica, menciona que los estándares específicos de Go están pendientes
assertions.check: response acknowledges Go standards are [PENDIENTE] OR proceeds with general Go best practices
assertions.pass_when: Response either states that Go-specific standards are [PENDIENTE] in stacks/go/STACK.md and proceeds with general Go conventions, OR applies idiomatic Go patterns without requiring the note if standards are already defined.
assertions.fail_when: Response proceeds without any acknowledgment of the stack standards status and without applying any Go-idiomatic patterns.
